title: Qix Clone: Ink & Exposure
pitch: Claim 75% of a shrinking field before the ink runs out or the Qix catches your line. Fast claims are safe but small; bold claims are big but slow to draw and lethal if cut off. One input scheme, two enemy types, synthesized-style feedback via GPU-rendered glow and shape — commit to a cut, and live with the exposure.
core_loop: Stand on safe claimed border, read Qix trajectory and Sparx patrol timing
Push into open space to start drawing a trail; every frame exposed is risk (Qix or Sparx can kill you)
Choose cut length/angle: short safe claim vs long bold claim; optionally hold Shift for a fast but uncontrollable dash
Reconnect trail to border: Board.commit_trail splits polygon via polygon_fill, claims the enclosed area, updates percent_claimed and score with a claim feedback beat
Escalate: Qix speed/turn-rate and Sparx count/speed ratchet up per level and via soft 20s timer pressure; repeat until area% target reached or lives exhausted
controls: Arrow keys / WASD: move head (4-directional intent)
Moving along claimed border = safe walking (auto)
Moving into unclaimed space = auto-drawing trail (no separate draw button)
Left Shift: fast draw (1.5x speed, brief no-turn commitment window) - risk/reward knob
quality_bar: Delta-time movement everywhere: all gamelib update(dt) calls use seconds-as-float from one Clock.tick in main.py; no entity reads wall-clock directly
Strict layering with zero circular imports: core -> states -> {gamelib, render}; gamelib and render never import each other, states, or pygame/moderngl types beyond primitives
gamelib is pure Python and headless-testable (dataclasses/functions only, no pygame/moderngl imports); render only consumes primitive tuples/floats/colors, never gamelib objects
Explicit state machine with StateID enum and State Protocol (on_enter/on_exit/handle_events/update/render/next); no scattered boolean mode flags
All persistence and shader loading confined to paths under the game directory (Path(__file__).parent-relative); no network access, shell commands, or dynamic code execution
Collision/geometry logic centralized in grid_geometry.py and polygon_fill.py and reused by Board, Player, Sparx, QixEnemy to keep DRY

files.name: main.py
files.purpose: Single entry point: initializes pygame + GL context, builds GameContext, runs the fixed-timestep loop calling StateMachine.handle_events/update/render

files.name: settings.py
files.purpose: Static constants: window size, FPS, colors, speeds, area% targets per level

files.name: core/game_context.py
files.purpose: Mutable world handle passed to every state; holds gl context, renderer, input state, score, rng seed

files.name: core/state_machine.py
files.purpose: Drives current State: dispatches events/update/render and swaps state based on State.next()

files.name: core/input.py
files.purpose: Pure translation of pygame events into an InputState dataclass; single point of pygame.event coupling

files.name: core/clock.py
files.purpose: Thin wrapper documenting the dt convention (seconds as float) used across the codebase; helper for fixed max-dt clamping

files.name: states/base.py
files.purpose: State Protocol and StateID enum defining explicit game states and transition contract

files.name: states/menu_state.py
files.purpose: Title/menu screen: shows instructions, waits for confirm to start PlayingState

files.name: states/playing_state.py
files.purpose: Core gameplay glue: updates Board/Player/QixEnemy/Sparx from gamelib, converts their primitive geometry to Renderer draw calls, handles pause/death transitions

files.name: states/paused_state.py
files.purpose: Freezes gameplay update, renders paused overlay, resumes on unpause input

files.name: states/game_over_state.py
files.purpose: Shows final score/area%, writes high score via persistence, returns to MenuState on confirm

files.name: gamelib/grid_geometry.py
files.purpose: Pure geometry primitives shared across board/player/enemies: point-in-polygon, segment intersection, polygon area
public_api: segment_intersects(a1, a2, b1, b2) -> Point | None

files.name: gamelib/polygon_fill.py
files.purpose: Splits claimed border polygon by a committed trail path and triangulates resulting fill regions for rendering/area calc

files.name: gamelib/board.py
files.purpose: Owns claimed polygons, border, and active trail; resolves claim commits and exposes percent_claimed

files.name: gamelib/player.py
files.purpose: Player entity with dt-based movement constrained to border-walk or trail-draw modes per Qix rules

files.name: gamelib/enemies.py
files.purpose: QixEnemy erratic bouncing polygon confined to unclaimed space, speed/turn-rate scale with level

files.name: gamelib/sparx.py
files.purpose: Sparx enemies patrolling the claimed border at increasing speed/count with level escalation

files.name: gamelib/scoring.py
files.purpose: Tracks score, lives, and area-claim rewards (bonus for fast-draw risk)

files.name: render/gl_context.py
files.purpose: Creates the moderngl.Context bound to the pygame OPENGL surface

files.name: render/shaders.py
files.purpose: Loads and compiles vertex/fragment shader pairs from render/shaders_src/ into moderngl programs

files.name: render/line_batch.py
files.purpose: GPU line-strip drawing primitive for border/trail visuals

files.name: render/polygon_mesh.py
files.purpose: GPU triangle mesh primitive for filled claimed-area visuals

files.name: render/renderer.py
files.purpose: High-level draw API consuming only primitive tuples/colors; wraps LineBatch/PolygonMesh and marker circles, manages frame begin/end

files.name: persistence/highscores.py
files.purpose: Reads/writes local JSON high-score file inside the game directory only

files.name: render/text_renderer.py
files.purpose: Added during refinement: Root cause of the "anonymous dots" menu: MenuState/GameOverState only ever called renderer.draw_marker() for title/instruction text (dots), since the Renderer had no actual text-drawing capability at

dependencies.reason: Efficient construction and per-frame upload of vertex buffers (triangulated fill polygons, batched line strips) to moderngl VBOs
